---
title: "British World War I / Lloyd George"
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status: draft corpus"}
This case has source text, manifest registration, segmentation, and MIPVU
worklists, but no reviewed annotations, support rating, or reliability sample
yet. Source text is English OCR from public-domain Internet Archive scans and
requires OCR review before publication-grade claims.
:::

## Analytical focus

The British World War I case examines Lloyd George-centered democratic wartime
rhetoric about sacrifice, endurance, national service, liberty, small nations,
military autocracy, victory, and international justice.

## Corpus boundary

This is a compact Lloyd George public corpus, not a general British World War I
archive. The initial draft selects four speeches from 1914-1917 because the
pre-v1 expansion review identified this case as a high-value limiting/control
test for democratic wartime mobilization and sacrifice language without a
genocidal enemy-destruction frame.

## Draft artifacts

- [Corpus analysis](../../analysis/corpus-analysis.md)
- [Diachronic analysis](../../analysis/diachronic-analysis.md)
- [Metaphor-system evolution](../../analysis/cluster-evolution.md)
- [Critical metaphor analysis](../../analysis/critical-metaphor-analysis.md)
- [Rhetorical and genre analysis](../../analysis/rhetorical-genre-analysis.md)
- [Absence and agency analysis](../../analysis/absence-agency-analysis.md)

Return to [all cases](../../../../case-selection.qmd).
